article_meta.title: Washington DC Trump celebrates US' long history giving foreign enemies 'hell' at massive military parade The military pa

reasoning: The model exhibits several failure patterns. It uses invented bias type labels not from the approved list (e.g., 'Positive framing bias', 'Uncritical reporting', 'Selective context', 'Platforming without scrutiny', 'False equivalence/Minimization'). It commits attribution rule violations by flagging quoted text from Trump as author bias (items 4 and 5). However, item 3 correctly identifies genuinely loaded author prose ('illegal aliens who flooded the nation', 'riots and protests') though with the wrong bias type label. The model also misses some bias instances while flagging some questionable ones.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 4 and 5 flag Trump's quoted statements as bias. Item 1 flags the headline which partially uses quoted language ('hell'). Item 2 uses 'celebrated by the administration' which is attributed language. Item 3 is the strongest — 'illegal aliens who flooded the nation' and 'riots and protests' are the author's own prose choices. So there are 2-3 clear attribution violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Item 3 contains genuinely biased author prose ('flooded the nation', 'illegal aliens', framing protests as 'riots'). Items 4 and 5 flag quoted text. Item 1 flags the headline which is partly the author's framing but partly quotes. Item 2 is debatable — the author does pass along the claim uncritically, but the article does note it came from 'the administration.' Several items are questionable or wrong.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model did catch the key loaded language in item 3 ('illegal aliens who flooded the nation during the Biden administration'). It arguably missed the overall slant/tone of the article which is largely favorable to the parade and Trump administration. The phrase 'patriotic event' in the author's prose is also loaded. But the biggest bias instance (the loaded immigration language) was caught.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All five bias type labels are invented and not from the approved list. 'Positive framing bias', 'Uncritical reporting', 'Selective context', 'Platforming without scrutiny', 'False equivalence/Minimization' are not valid types. The correct labels would have been things like Word Choice, Slant, Spin, Unsubstantiated Claims, etc.
